english: — nor-´dzin (nor 'dzin) po. the earth. — nor-rdzás (nor rdzas) = nor I., 1. B. and col. — nór-lha (nor lha) = ku-be-ra, god of riches; there are eight such gods. II. v. sub nór-ba (nor ba).

to err, to make a mistake, to commit a fault, gas ´ńul nór-ro (gas 'ngul nor ro) it is wrong (to write it) with the prefix γ Gram.; nor soń (nor song) it is a mistake, I (thou, he etc.) am wrong; k´a, lág-pa, lam nor soń (kha, lag pa, lam nor song), it was a slip of the tongue, I got hold of the wrong thing, I lost my way; to stray, dé-las ´di-ru (de las 'di ru) from one thing to another Thgy.; mi-nór-ba (mi nor ba), ma-nór-ba (ma nor ba), nor-ba-méd-pa (nor ba med pa) infallible, not liable to fail, e.g. of a charm; where one cannot miss or go wrong, lam; mi-nór-bar (mi nor bar), strictly according to prescription or direction. — nór-ba (nor ba), nór-pa (nor pa) Cs. 1. a wanderer, from the right way. 2. an error, a mistake. — nor-´khrúl (nor 'khrul) id., frq.; nór-ra-re (nor ra re) Sch.: he might possibly be mistaken.

(मणि) 1. jewel, gem, precious stone, nór-bu-ċan (nor bu can) adorned with jewels, set with precious stones; nór-bu-pa (nor bu pa), nór-bu-mk´an (nor bu mkhan) Cs. a jeweler, a connoisseur of gems; nór-bu-_preń-ba (nor bu 'phreng ba) a rosary or chaplet composed of precious stones; also as title of a book; nór-bu rín-po-čé (nor bu rin po che), चिन्तामणि, a very costly jewel; also jewel, par excellence, a fabulous precious stone, the possession of which procures inexhaustible riches; acc. to Wdk. 488, it has the shape of an oval fruit of the size of a large lemon. — 2. a noun personal, or family name, much in use. — 3. gen. pronounced *nór-ru* (*nor ru*), *nór-ro* (*nor ro*), good, excellent, noble, e.g. mi, Bal., Pur.
nór-so (nor so), nór-so-ċan (nor so can), Wdk. 173, 11; 182, 4?
to agree, to come to terms Cs.
Ssk., Ficus indica, = byań-ċub-śiń (byang cub shing).

a secondary form of nág-pa (nag pa), of rare occurrence, 1. black; γnag-sbágs (gnag sbags) sooty Sch.; γnag-pyúgs (gnag phyugs) black cattle, esp. the yak; γnag rta lug γsum (gnag rta lug gsum) cattle, horses, and sheep, these three; γnag-kyú (gnag khyu) a herd of cattle; γnag-rdzi (gnag rdzi) a keeper of cattle, cow-herd; γnag-lhás (gnag lhas) an enclosure for cattle. — 2. fig. black-hearted, wicked, impious. — 3. (looking black upon) frowning; Glr. fol. 96: sems śin-tu γnág-par byuń (sems shin tu gnag par byung) (notwithstanding their friendly appearance) they had a spite against each other in their hearts. — 4. sbst. misfortune, grief, affliction, pain, γnág-pa dań ldán-pa (gnag pa dang ldan pa) unfortunate, unhappy Stg.; *nag-ċan* (*nag can*) W. cruel, tormenting; *nag stán-pa* (*nag stan pa*) Ld. to torture, to torment. — 5. Sch.: (well) considered, (carefully) weighed in the mind; v. however brnág-pa (brnag pa).

I. vb., pf. γnań(s) (gnang(s)), imp. γnoń (gnong), B., C. (in W. stsál-ba (stsal ba) is gen. used for γnáń-ba (gnang ba)) 1. to give, resp., i.e. only used when a person of higher rank gives or is asked to give; cf. _búl-ba ('bul ba); *dág-la dá-wa čig-gi ṗog kyáb-rog náń-wa žu* (*dag la da wa cig gi pog kyab rog nang wa zhu*) C. please, have the kindness to give me my month's pay; sometimes it is preceded by a pleon. rjés-su (rjes su), Cs., to bestow, to confer, upon, frq.; to commit to, to place under a person's care, e.g. a pupil (resp. for γtód-pa (gtod pa)) Mil.; to grant, to concede, what has been asked, γnáń-du γsol (gnang du gsol) (ancient lit.), γnáń-ba žu (gnang ba zhu) (later lit.) I request you to grant; skur-γnáń mdzád-pa mk´yén-mk´yén (skur gnang mdzad pa mkhyen mkhyen) I beg you for the favour of sending me... (in modern letters); to allow, permit, approve of, assent to, γśégs-par γnáń-ńo (gshegs par gnang ngo) he accepted the invitation, he promised to come Dzl.; bdag ráb-tu _byúń-ba(r) γnoń žig (bdag rab tu 'byung ba(r) gnong zhig) allow me to take (holy) orders, to become a priest Dzl.; bdag ni sbyín-pa žig byéd-kyis γnoń žig (bdag ni sbyin pa zhig byed kyis gnong zhig) allow of my making a donation Dzl.; de bžín-du γnáń-ńo (de bzhin du gnang ngo) yes, I permit it Dzl.; yid bžín-du γnáń-ńo (yid bzhin du gnang ngo) we allow it; do according to your pleasure! — ċi γnań (ci gnang) v. ċi (ci) I., 4. — In a looser sense: blón-por γnáń-ńo (blon por gnang ngo) he appointed him his minister; mi γnáń-ba (mi gnang ba) to forbid, prohibit, čos byar mi γnáń-baí k´rims bċas (chos byar mi gnang ba'i khrims bcas) he published a prohibitory law concerning the exercise of religion Glr.; (bkas) ma γnań ((bkas) ma gnang) Pth. he refused it, declined to grant it, byon-du ma γnań (byon du ma gnang) he refused
